summary: Planned comparative study of WT vs barstar-D39A barnase-barstar binding. The barstar D39A interface hotspot is expected to weaken binding; this bundle packages the methods, provenance roles, and decision log for an auditable study, with the binding direction anchored to the experimental literature.

effect.basis: literature-calibrated (Schreiber & Fersht 1995); MD plan provides consistency evidence only.

citations.note: Barstar D39A weakens barnase-barstar binding by 3-5 orders of magnitude in Kd.

limitations: Dry-run methods bundle; no production trajectories are shipped.
Short MD cannot establish an absolute binding free energy; the direction is literature-calibrated.
